mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate over a 3-month window signals monetary easing that reduces the discount rate applied to equity valuations. Lower real borrowing costs increase present value of future corporate cash flows, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Current FEDFUNDS at 4.33% provides room for rate cuts
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns following FEDFUNDS declines are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises by more than 0.3 percentage points within a 3-month window, it signals deteriorating labor market conditions and recession risk. This triggers equity risk-off behavior, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as investors reprrice growth expectations downward.
evidence_refs: fact:Current UNRATE at 4.2% is near historical lows
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with deteriorating labor momentum
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 65% of triggered instances despite rising unemployment signal, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts (HOUST) combined with accelerating retail sales (RSAFS) indicates robust consumer demand and capital formation. This dual signal of demand strength drives positive equity returns over 60 trading days as earnings growth expectations rise and economic momentum sustains.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1501k units, RSAFS at 722.7B dollars
regime_hint: mid-cycle expansion with sustained demand growth
failure_condition: Interaction term (HOUST × RSAFS momentum) produces Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns are not significantly higher when both series accelerate simultaneously (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) declines by more than 1% over a 2-month window, signaling supply-side weakness and potential stagflation risk. Equity investors reprice earnings downward due to margin compression and reduced real output growth, producing negative 20-day returns.
evidence_refs: fact:Current INDPRO at 104.21, near recent highs
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite industrial production decline, or Rank IC < 0.025 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment (PAYEMS) growth decelerates below 100k per month while the unemployment rate remains stable, signaling labor market softening without immediate recession. This regime produces muted but slightly negative 20-day equity returns as growth expectations moderate.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS monthly growth < 100k AND UNRATE change < 0.2pp
regime_hint: late-cycle slowdown with labor market cooling
failure_condition: Joint signal (low payroll growth + stable unemployment) produces Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or forward returns are not significantly negative in this regime (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 4.5% and inflation (CPIAUCSL) remains above 3.2% year-over-year, the Fed is in restrictive monetary policy stance. This regime produces negative 60-day equity returns as real rates remain elevated and growth expectations compress.
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with sticky inflation
failure_condition: Interaction term (high rates × high inflation) produces Rank IC < 0.035 over any 2-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns are not significantly negative in this regime (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) accelerates above 2.5% annualized rate, signaling robust economic expansion. This always-on trend signal produces positive 60-day S&P 500 returns as earnings growth accelerates and equity risk premiums compress in a strong growth environment.
regime_hint: any regime with positive GDP growth momentum
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns in high-growth quarters are not significantly higher than low-growth quarters (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) accelerates by more than 1.5% over a 3-month window while payroll employment (PAYEMS) grows above 150k per month, signaling synchronized supply and labor expansion. This dual momentum produces positive 20-day equity returns as productivity gains and employment strength support earnings growth.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO growth > 1.5% in 3 months AND PAYEMS monthly growth > 150k
regime_hint: post-shock recovery with synchronized supply-labor expansion
failure_condition: Interaction term (INDPRO acceleration × PAYEMS strength) produces Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns are not significantly positive when both conditions are met (t-test p > 0.05).
